title: Embargoed Race-Aware CV And Fold-Safe Encoders
summary: The current feature set already uses race/driver history heavily, so optimistic CV is a real risk if adjacent laps from the same race narrative leak across folds.
rationale: The current feature set already uses race/driver history heavily, so optimistic CV is a real risk if adjacent laps from the same race narrative leak across folds. Purged or gap-based splits are specifically designed for labels built from future information and should make model selection more leaderboard-aligned.
implementation_hint: Split by ordered `Year-Race` with a lap-gap embargo around validation races; recompute every target/stat encoding, prior, and aggregate inside each fold only; compare current CV to embargoed CV before trusting small gains.
expected_effect: Likely lowers local CV a bit but improves signal-to-noise when selecting features and blends, reducing false positives from leakage-driven tweaks.
risk: This may expose that some current gains are not real, and fewer effective folds can increase variance of local estimates.
